name: Phase 2 — Frontend alignment: clamp values, fix HVAC editor seed, ship readings poller
description: Five stories that bring the HACS frontend into alignment with the post-Phase-1 backend. (1) Thermostat card slider clamps to 1-3, not 1-5 — backend rejects 4/5 silently today. (2) Water-heater insulation_factor accepts the canonical [0.01, 0.05] range with default 0.03; matches the new server-side validator from Phase 1.4. (3) HVAC editor seeds from `/api/v1/preferences` instead of `appliance.config`, and exposes `time_away` / `time_home` fields. (4) Python integration polls the configured climate entity every 5 min and POSTs to `/api/v1/readings` so the optimizer finally has data. (5) `getSchedule` TS type drops `'none'` from its source union — the post-Phase-1 backend never returns it. PRECONDITION: Phase 0 and Phase 1 must be merged and the API redeployed before this PRD runs. Story 4 is the largest; the rest are small.
env_policy: Frontend repo. All TypeScript tests use vitest + happy-dom + mocked fetch — never hit api.hungrymachines.io. Python integration tests use unittest.mock against aiohttp; never hit a live HA. No env vars required for any Verify step. The `npm test`, `npm run build`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `pytest custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/` commands are the canonical Verify primitives.

stories.title: Thermostat card savings slider clamps to 1-3
stories.description: Today `src/cards/thermostat-card.ts:325-329` ships a slider with `min=1, max=5` and `_clampLevel` (line 219) clamps to 1-5. Backend `preferences.py:43-47` rejects anything outside `{1, 2, 3}` with a 400. The card's update is `.catch(() => {})` so the slider silently stops persisting at 4/5. Trivial fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit `src/cards/thermostat-card.ts:325-329`: change the `<input type='range'>` element's `max="5"` to `max="3"`. The min stays at 1, step stays at 1.
Edit `src/cards/thermostat-card.ts:219-225`: change `_clampLevel` to clamp to `[1, 3]` instead of `[1, 5]`. Default fallback for non-finite stays at 3 (the existing midpoint of the new range).

Update the constraint editor's HVAC tab (`src/ui/constraint-editor.ts`) so its slider also matches: `min="1" max="3" step="1"`. Confirm the existing validator at `_validate` for `savings_level` already rejects values outside [1, 3] (it does — line 401-404).

Tests: extend `tests/thermostat-card.test.ts` (create file if absent following the existing card-test pattern in tests/savings-card.test.ts). Cases: (a) slider element rendered with max='3'; (b) `_clampLevel(5)` returns 3; (c) `_clampLevel(0)` returns 1; (d) dragging to 4 (synthesised input event) is clamped to 3 before the debounced PUT fires.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm test` all pass. `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm run build` succeeds. `grep -F 'max="5"' src/cards/thermostat-card.ts | wc -l` returns 0. `grep -E 'max="3"' src/cards/thermostat-card.ts src/ui/constraint-editor.ts | wc -l` returns at least 1 in each file.
stories.notes: Tightened the savings slider to backend's [1,3] range. src/cards/thermostat-card.ts: max="5" -> max="3" (lines 325-329) and _clampLevel upper bound 5 -> 3 (line 223). src/ui/constraint-editor.ts already used max="3" with [1,3] validation from a prior story; verified, no edit needed. tests/thermostat-card.test.ts: updated existing debounce test to use in-range value (2) instead of 4 so it still asserts the round-trip; added three new cases — slider element renders max="3", _clampLevel boundary table (5->3, 4->3, 0->1, -1->1, NaN->3, 2->2), and a clamp-on-drag case where a synthesised input event with value '4' results in a PUT body of {savings_level: 3} and the visible .slider-value shows '3'. 95 vitest cases pass (was 92; +3 new). npm run build clean. Verify greps: max="5" count 0, max="3" count 2 (one per file).

stories.title: Water-heater insulation_factor accepts 0.01-0.05 with default 0.03
stories.description: Form at `src/ui/appliance-form.ts:213, 295, 605-617` defaults `insulation_factor: '0.5'` and validates `[0, 1]`. Docs and the post-Phase-1 backend validator say `[0.01, 0.05]`. Default 0.5 is 10× the documented max. Bring the form into alignment.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit `src/ui/appliance-form.ts:213` (the water_heater branch of `_defaultValues`): change `insulation_factor: '0.5'` to `insulation_factor: '0.03'`.
Edit `src/ui/appliance-form.ts:295` (the `reqInRange('insulation_factor', 0, 1, ...)` line in `_validate`): change to `reqInRange('insulation_factor', 0.01, 0.05, 'Must be 0.01-0.05')`.
Edit the input element at lines 605-617: change `min="0"` to `min="0.01"`, `max="1"` to `max="0.05"`, `step="0.05"` to `step="0.005"` (so users can pick 0.01, 0.015, 0.02, … 0.05).

Update the visible label text 'Insulation factor (0–1)' on line 604 to 'Insulation factor (0.01–0.05)'. Add a single small `<small>` hint underneath the input: 'Lower = better insulated. 0.03 is typical for a residential tank.' Use the existing `.label-text` styling.

Tests: extend `tests/appliance-form.test.ts` (or create following existing patterns). Cases: (a) opening the form, picking 'Water heater', the insulation_factor input shows default value '0.03'; (b) entering '0.5' fails validation with 'Must be 0.01-0.05'; (c) entering '0.025' validates clean; (d) submitting with 0.03 results in `appliances.create` being called with `config.insulation_factor === 0.03`.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm test` all pass. `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm run build` succeeds. `grep -F "insulation_factor: '0.5'" src/ui/appliance-form.ts | wc -l` returns 0. `grep -F 'reqInRange.*insulation_factor.*0.01.*0.05' src/ui/appliance-form.ts | wc -l` returns at least 1 (or with regex flexibility, the values 0.01 and 0.05 must both appear in the same call).
stories.notes: Brought the water-heater insulation_factor form field into alignment with the post-Phase-1 backend validator [0.01, 0.05] (default 0.03). src/ui/appliance-form.ts: default '0.5' -> '0.03' in _defaultValues (line 213); reqInRange bounds 0..1 -> 0.01..0.05 with new error 'Must be 0.01-0.05' in _validate (line 295); input element min='0' -> '0.01', max='1' -> '0.05', step='0.05' -> '0.005', .value fallback '0.5' -> '0.03', label text '(0–1)' -> '(0.01–0.05)' (lines 603-617); added <small class='label-text'> hint 'Lower = better insulated. 0.03 is typical for a residential tank.' beneath the input, before the field-error slot, so the hint is always visible while the inline error sits below it. tests/appliance-form.test.ts: added 4 new cases — default 0.03 with min/max/step attrs, '0.5' fails validation with the exact error string and disables Add, '0.025' validates clean and enables Add, default 0.03 submit posts config.insulation_factor === 0.03 (numeric, not string). Vitest 99/99 pass (was 95; +4). npm run build clean. Verify greps: insulation_factor: '0.5' count 0; reqInRange…0.01…0.05 count 1.

stories.title: HVAC editor seeds from /api/v1/preferences and exposes time_away/time_home
stories.description: `src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts:827-834` hands `appliance.config` to the constraint editor as `currentConstraints`. For HVAC, that config is `{hvac_type, home_size_sqft}` — never the user's preferences. The editor's `_seedValues` (constraint-editor.ts:283-323) silently falls back to hard-coded `72/3/auto` every open. Also: `time_away` and `time_home` are part of the API but the editor never lets the user set them. Fix both at once: seed from `_preferences` (panel already loads them at line 633-641) and expose the two time fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit `src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts:827-834` (`_openEditor` method): when `type === 'hvac'`, pass `this._preferences ?? {}` as `currentConstraints` instead of `appliance.config`. For all other appliance types, keep `appliance.config` (current behaviour). Add a one-line comment explaining the divergence.

Edit `src/ui/constraint-editor.ts:283-323` (`_seedValues`, hvac branch): add reads for `time_away` and `time_home` from the constraints object using the same string-coerce helper. Default both to `''` if missing (NOT to '08:00'/'17:00' — the API has its own defaults; an empty string means 'don't change this field on submit').

Edit `src/ui/constraint-editor.ts:_buildPayload` hvac branch (line 451-466): if `_values['time_away']` is non-empty, include it in the payload; same for `time_home`. Both still optional. Also: keep the existing hourly_low/hourly_high fields exactly as-is.

Edit `src/ui/constraint-editor.ts:_validate` hvac branch (around line 398-426): add HH:MM regex validation for `time_away` and `time_home` only when they are non-empty (empty = 'no change' and is valid).

Edit the HVAC render block in `src/ui/constraint-editor.ts:710-758` to add two `<input type='text' pattern='\d{2}:\d{2}'>` fields for `time_away` and `time_home`, with helper text 'Time you typically leave home (HH:MM, leave blank to keep current)' and 'Time you typically return home (HH:MM, leave blank to keep current)'. Hide both inputs when `_hourlyEnabled` is true (hourly bands override these).

Tests: extend `tests/constraint-editor.test.ts`. Cases: (a) opening the HVAC editor with `currentConstraints = {base_temperature: 70, savings_level: 2, optimization_mode: 'cool', time_away: '07:30', time_home: '18:00'}` shows all five fields populated; (b) submitting with time_away changed and others untouched calls `updatePreferences` with `{base_temperature, savings_level, optimization_mode, time_away: '06:30', hourly_high_temps_f: null, hourly_low_temps_f: null}` (time_home omitted because empty); (c) entering 'invalid' in time_away shows 'Use HH:MM' error and disables Save; (d) when the user enables hourly bands, the time_away/time_home inputs are hidden in the DOM. Update tests/panel-schedules.test.ts to assert that `_openEditor` for HVAC passes `_preferences`, not `appliance.config`.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm test` all pass. `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm run build` succeeds. `grep -F 'time_away' src/ui/constraint-editor.ts | wc -l` returns at least 4 (seed, validate, payload, render). `grep -F 'this._preferences' src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts | wc -l` returns at least 2 (load + pass to editor).

stories.title: Python integration polls climate entity every 5 min and POSTs readings
stories.description: The HACS integration never calls `/api/v1/readings`. Without a stream of readings, the backend's thermal-model fitter has no observations and the optimizer runs blind on defaults forever. Add a polling task that reads the user's configured `climate.*` entity every 5 minutes and pushes a single reading. The poller must tolerate missing entities, missing attributes, and 4xx responses without crashing. This is the largest story in this PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Read `custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py` and `scheduler.py` end-to-end first to understand how the existing scheduler task is registered (it likely uses `async_track_time_change` or similar). Match that pattern.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/readings.py` exposing one async function: `async def push_current_reading(hass, entry) -> bool`. It (a) reads the climate entity ID from `entry.options.get(CONF_CLIMATE_ENTITY) or entry.data.get(CONF_CLIMATE_ENTITY)` — return False if absent; (b) fetches `hass.states.get(entity_id)` — return False if absent; (c) builds the reading dict from state attrs: `indoor_temp = state.attributes.get('current_temperature')`, `target_temp = state.attributes.get('temperature')`, `indoor_humidity = state.attributes.get('current_humidity')`. The `hvac_state` is derived from `state.state.upper()` mapped to one of `{HEAT, COOL, OFF, FAN}` — anything else maps to `OFF`; (d) skip the entire push if `indoor_temp is None`; (e) gets a token via `auth.current_token(hass, entry)` and POSTs `{readings: [reading]}` to `f'{API_BASE_URL}/api/v1/readings'`. On 401, trigger reauth (same pattern as scheduler.py:48-54). On 4xx other than 401, log a warning and return False. On 201/200, return True.

Wire into `__init__.py async_setup_entry`: register a 5-minute interval task using HA's `async_track_time_interval(hass, lambda _now: hass.async_create_task(readings.push_current_reading(hass, entry)), timedelta(minutes=5))`. Store the unsub callable on `hass.data[DOMAIN]['readings_unsub']` and call it in `async_unload_entry`.

Tests: create `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_readings.py`. Cases: (a) climate entity present with valid attrs → push_current_reading returns True, aiohttp post called once with body `{readings: [{timestamp, indoor_temp: 72.5, hvac_state: 'COOL', target_temp: 72.0, ...}]}`; (b) climate entity absent → returns False, no post; (c) entity present but `current_temperature` is None → returns False, no post; (d) post returns 401 → entry.async_start_reauth called; (e) post returns 429 → returns False, no crash; (f) state is 'cool' (lowercase) → `hvac_state` is 'COOL'; (g) state is 'auto' → `hvac_state` is 'OFF' (unmapped fallback). Mock aiohttp the same way scheduler tests do.

Update `custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py` and `readings.py` to share a small helper for token fetch + reauth on 401 — extract `_authenticated_post` or similar if it'll cut code duplication. Do not over-engineer.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && pytest custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/ -v` all tests including the new ones pass. `grep -F 'push_current_reading' custom_components/hungry_machines/ -r | wc -l` returns at least 3 (definition + import in __init__ + test imports). `grep -F 'async_track_time_interval' custom_components/hungry_machines/__init__.py | wc -l` returns at least 1. `grep -F '/api/v1/readings' custom_components/hungry_machines/readings.py | wc -l` returns at least 1.

Bump manifest version (e.g. 0.3.4 → 0.4.0 — readings poller is a real feature, not a patch). Add CHANGELOG line: 'feat: poll the configured climate entity every 5 minutes and push readings to the API; the optimizer now has data to learn from.'

stories.title: ApplianceSchedule TS type drops 'none' and matches post-Phase-1 backend
stories.description: After Phase 1.3, `GET /api/v1/appliances/{id}/schedule` always returns `source: 'optimization'` or `'defaults'`. The TS type at `src/api/appliances.ts:35-41` already says `'optimization' | 'defaults'` — that part is already correct. But the runtime placeholder behaviour is gone, so we should also remove any defensive frontend code that handles `'none'`.
acceptanceCriteria: Search the frontend src/ tree for any code that compares to the literal string `'none'` in the context of a schedule source. Expected hit: zero in src/api/, possibly one in src/panel/ or src/ui/. Document each hit before changing.

If `getSchedule` is called from anywhere in the frontend, audit it: does it correctly handle `source: 'defaults'` (e.g. show 'no optimization yet' rather than 'error')? If not, fix the call site. If `getSchedule` is called from nowhere, leave the wrapper for future use but add a `// post-Phase-1 contract: source is always 'optimization' or 'defaults'` comment above its declaration.

Add a tests/api-appliances.test.ts case (extend if file exists): mocked fetch returning `{appliance_id: 'a1', date: '2026-05-06', schedule: {intervals: [], high_temps: [], low_temps: []}, savings_pct: 0, source: 'defaults'}` — the wrapper resolves the typed shape without TS errors, and `result.source === 'defaults'`.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npm test` passes. `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` clean. `grep -F "'none'" src/ -r --include='*.ts' | grep -v node_modules | wc -l` returns 0 in any schedule-source context (the literal `'none'` may legitimately appear elsewhere — this verify is loose; the audit step above is the real check).
